full_prompt: Design a monolithic 7075-T6 aluminum rear driven wheel hub for a collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark 2026 (FSCH) Class 1 CV vehicle with a brazed spec 13-inch wheel and the FS Rules 2026 base (tube OD 25.4 x 2.4 mm steel for chassis datum, 95th-%ile driver template, 20 mm gasoline intake restrictor, IA 7.5g avg / 20g peak), as supplemented by the FSCH Handbook 2026 v1.0 tight autocross emphasis. The hub is a single forged and machined billet part (no inserts, no welded studs; all five wheel studs are separate and not part of this CAD prompt; treat the hub as one solid body with integral stud through-bores for press-fit studs). Geometry: outer wheel-mount flange 150 mm OD x 10 mm thick with a 5-stud pattern on a 100 mm PCD (each bore 14 mm for M12 press-fit stud), integral pilot diameter 56 mm x 6 mm long for wheel centering, integral brake-rotor mount flange 135 mm PCD with 8x M8 (9 mm clearance) on the inner face, central bearing bore 55 mm ID x 70 mm axial length for twin tapered roller bearings (bearing shoulder step mid-length, 60 mm ID x 4 mm shoulder), integral splined bore for 26-tooth 30-mm-module half-shaft spline over 45 mm length. Overall bounding envelope 160 mm OD x 90 mm length. Material 7075-T6 (E 71.7 GPa, nu 0.33, density 2810 kg/m^3, yield 503 MPa, UTS 572 MPa, endurance limit 159 MPa at 5e8 cycles rotating bending per MIL-HDBK-5J). Vehicle mass 260 kg + 68 kg 95th-%ile driver = 328 kg, with 50% rear weight distribution giving 164 kg on rear axle, 82 kg per rear wheel; tire loaded radius 230 mm; peak engine torque at countershaft sprocket 50 N-m at chain, multiplied by final drive ratio 4.0 = 200 N-m at hub per corner; braking peak 1.5g longitudinal produces 1206 N horizontal at the tire contact. Three load cases. Load Case 1 (combined cornering): 1.6g lateral (cornering) + 2.0g vertical bump = static vertical 804 N x 2 = 1608 N vertical plus 1286 N lateral, applied at the wheel-mount pilot diameter at 230 mm radius (tire contact patch offset), the bearing bore and half-shaft spline reacted. Load Case 2 (launch torque): 200 N-m drive torque applied at the spline with the wheel mount reacted as pure torsional; combined with 1.2g forward accel so 966 N longitudinal at tire contact. Load Case 3 (peak brake): 1500 N-m brake torque applied at the 8-hole brake mount flange while the wheel mount is reacted in rotation, combined with 1.5g deceleration forward force 1206 N longitudinal. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1 combined cornering, peak von Mises must not exceed 252 MPa (yield/2.0, standard collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark suspension SF); (R2) under LC2 launch, peak stress 252 MPa; (R3) under LC3 brake, peak stress 335 MPa (yield/1.5, brake peak transient per collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark rule brake SF); (R4) fatigue: at 10^7 cycles under cyclic cornering loading with stress range 30% of LC1 peak, local stress range at any spline root or stud bore must not exceed 159 MPa; (R5) a modal analysis must keep the first six natural frequencies above 500 Hz to avoid harmonic coupling with the 200 Hz first-order unbalance at 12000 rpm (max wheel speed at top speed 100 km/h on 0.46 m loaded diameter tires = 962 rpm, but hub measured at wheel bearing rate, so 500 Hz floor is conservative for autocross); (R6) hub mass must not exceed 0.85 kg, typical collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark-competitive ceiling; (R7) maximum tilt of the wheel plane (wheel-mount flange flatness deviation) under LC1 must not exceed 0.15 mm to preserve camber control. Thermal, fluid, buckling, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver STEP AP242 and Parasolid plus report.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic 7075-T6 aluminum rear driven wheel hub for a collegiate formula-class racecar (FSCH 2026, 13-inch wheel, tight autocross emphasis). The hub is a single forged-and-machined billet with an outer 5-stud wheel-mount flange, an integral pilot diameter, an inner 8-bolt brake-rotor mount flange, a central twin-tapered-bearing bore, and an internally splined half-shaft bore (26-tooth, 30-mm module). Design with yield/2.0 standard suspension SF and yield/1.5 brake-peak transient SF per the collegiate formula-class rulebook practice.

Envelope: ~160 mm OD x 90 mm length.

Load cases.
- LC1 combined cornering: 1608 N vertical + 1286 N lateral at the wheel-mount pilot at 230 mm tire radius (1.6 g lateral + 2 g vertical bump on 82 kg corner), bearing bore and spline reacted.
- LC2 launch torque: 200 N-m at the spline (50 N-m chain x 4.0 final drive), wheel mount reacted torsionally, plus 966 N longitudinal (1.2 g).
- LC3 peak brake: 1500 N-m brake torque at the 8-hole brake mount reacted against the wheel mount in rotation, plus 1206 N longitudinal (1.5 g decel).
Also check a 10^7-cycle fatigue spectrum on LC1 stress range, first six modes >500 Hz, and wheel-plane flatness deviation <0.15 mm under LC1.

prompt.title: FS Switzerland rear driven wheel hub (7075-T6)
prompt.brief: Monolithic billet hub with 5-stud wheel pattern, 8-bolt brake mount, twin-bearing bore, splined drive shaft; FSCH 2026 + FS Rules 2026.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: yield/2.0 collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark suspension

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Brake-duty heating handled at rotor not hub.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Hub is dominated by fatigue + strength, not buckling.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Spline wear and Hertzian contact fatigue

eval_coverage: collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark suspension 2.0 SF + brake 1.5 SF differentiation
